project.title: Adaptor molecule of DNA methylation in IECs
project.projectDescription: We previously reported that gut microbiota induce de novo DNA methylation of the TLR4 gene in intestinal epithelial cells. Since DNA methyltransferase (DNMT) 3 mediates the transfer of methyl groups to any gene undergoing de novo methylation, the question of how gut microbiota induce the recruitment of DNMT3 to specific genes, including TLR4, remains to be addressed. In this study, we tried to identify an adaptor molecule that recruits DNMT3b to the TLR4 gene by comparing expression of DNMT3-interacting proteins between IEC lines with hypermethylated and hypomethylated TLR4 gene.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: Nuclear extracts prepared from HCT116 and SW480 cells were immunoprecipitated with anti-DNMT3b antibody. Immunoprecipitated proteins were separated by sodium dodecyl sulfate polyacrylamide gel electrophoresis and silver-stained. The stained bands were excised from the gel, destained, reduced, and alkylated, followed by tryptic digestion. After drying using a centrifugal evaporator, the product was dissolved in 0.1 % formic acid for LC-MS/MS analysis using Advance LC and Q Exactive.
project.dataProcessingProtocol: The data obtained were analyzed using Proteome Discoverer 2.1 software to search against the NCBI database using Sequent HT.

labPIs.title: Dr

experimentTypes.name: Affinity purification coupled with mass spectrometry proteomics

references.referenceLine: Narabayashi H, Koma C, Nakata K, Ikegami M, Nakanishi Y, Ogihara J, Tsuda M, Hosono A, Hanazawa S, Takahashi K. Gut microbiota-dependent adaptor molecule recruits DNA methyltransferase to the TLR4 gene in colonic epithelial cells to suppress inflammatory reactions. Front Mol Biosci. 2022 9:1005136

Characteristics[Specimen]: Nuclear extracts containing 200 µg of protein
Characteristics[CleavageAgent]: tryptic digestion using an In-Gel Tryptic Digestion Kit (Thermo Fisher Scientific)
Comment[Instrument]: Advance LC (AMR, Tokyo, Japan) and Q Exactive (Thermo Fishser Scientific)
Comment[EnrichmentMethod]: Immunoprecipitated proteins were separated by sodium dodecyl sulfate polyacrylamide gel electrophoresis and silver-stained using PierceTM Silver Stain for Mass Spectrometry (Thermo Fisher Scientific).
Comment[FractionationMethod]: The stained bands were excised from the gel
